item.title: China’s Moonshot unveils world’s largest open AI model, closing in on US rivals
item.description: The launch, which comes a month after Anthropic’s Fable and Mythos models were abruptly withdrawn by the U.S. government due to security concerns, underscores how quickly China’s open AI ecosystem is narrowing the gap with the most advanced ​U.S. systems.

item.title: Trump accuses China of 2020 election interference, contradicting US intel
item.description: The 25-minute prime-time address underscored Trump’s effort to make election security a central political issue ahead of November’s midterm elections, when his fellow Republicans will be defending their slender congressional majorities.

item.title: Iran tells Houthis to close Red Sea gateway if US hits power network, sources say
item.description: The idea has been discussed within the Islamic Republic’s leadership, and the message has been conveyed to Iran’s Houthi allies, two senior Iranian ‌sources and a regional source familiar with the matter said, speaking on condition of anonymity.

item.title: Bangladesh repatriates 171 nationals detained in Libya
item.description: The foreign ministry stated that most of the repatriated Bangladeshis had entered Libya illegally by sea, induced and assisted by human traffickers, with the intention of travelling to Europe.

item.title: Google required to open up to AI, search engine rivals under EU-mandated changes
item.description: The move by the European Commission, which acts as the EU competition enforcer, came six months after the regulator opened so-called specification proceedings to assist the world’s ​most popular internet search engine to comply with the Digital Markets Act.

item.title: PM for collective efforts to build prosperous, discrimination-free Bangladesh
item.description: The prime minister said his government is working to build a country where there will be no discrimination against any race or community.

item.title: Govt doesn’t believe in politics of division: LGRD Minister
item.description: The minister stated that the government’s goal is to work for the country’s development by bringing together people of all religions and communities under the spirit of Bangladeshi nationalism.

item.title: Bangladesh, European envoys review expanding strategic partnership, cooperation
item.description: The meeting featured fruitful discussions on the long-standing friendly relations between Bangladesh and its European partners, the growing economic partnership, and a range of issues of mutual interest.

item.title: Livestock sector loses BDT 770 million in floods in Chattogram’s five districts
item.description: The biggest blow from the floods has been on farm-based livestock production.

item.title: “Severity of climate change’s impacts depends on urban planning, disaster preparedness”
item.description: “The current situation results from both climate change and urban disaster management deficiencies. While climate change serves as a significant trigger, the severity of its impacts largely depends on urban planning, infrastructure management and disaster preparedness.”

item.title: UK, Saudi Arabia reaffirm aid partnership with first joint visit to Rohingya camps
item.description: Joint funding of $7.8 million by KSrelief and the UK government aims to support Rohingya refugees and host communities through UN agencies and local partners.

item.title: Online gambling, crypto fuel surge in suspicious financial transactions
item.description: The BFIU’s analysis states that the money from online gaming and gambling is not limited to bank or MFS accounts. There is an increasing trend of transferring these funds through cryptocurrency, hundi and other illicit channels for money laundering.

item.title: Lab tests uncover toxic contaminants in Bangladesh’s everyday staples
item.description: Health experts say the findings point to systemic failures in food safety oversight with potentially serious long-term public health consequences.

item.title: Britain nationalises British Steel in bid to safeguard domestic steelmaking
item.description: The government said bringing British Steel into public ownership was necessary to protect the UK’s national interest.

item.title: ABB seeks sweeping banking fee increases, faces business backlash
item.description: The bankers’ association has proposed new charges across 14 banking services, including higher loan-processing fees and fees for over-the-counter cash withdrawals exceeding three per month.

item.title: US launches multiple strikes on Iran, which retaliates against US targets in region
item.description: Hostilities have intensified since Iran said late on Saturday it had closed the Strait of Hormuz. Military operations are also keeping ships from transiting the vital artery.

item.title: Defending champion Argentina reaches World Cup final by beating England 2-1
item.description: Trailing 1-0 going into the 85th minute, Argentina rallied for a 2-1 victory over England with goals from Enzo Fernandez and substitute Lautaro Martinez.

item.title: Budget session of 13th Parliament concludes, 10 bills passed
item.description: The budget session formed 11 committees, including a special committee on constitutional amendments and reconstituted the Business Advisory Committee.

item.title: HSC Exams: 15 students expelled, over 20,000 absent across country
item.description: The average absence rate across these examinations was 2.67 percent.

item.title: Five more die from measles symptoms
item.description: With these latest figures, the total number of deaths from measles and measles-like symptoms this year has reached 771.

item.title: UK govt announces £355,000 in humanitarian assistance to support flood-affected people in Bangladesh
item.description: The UK contribution will provide affected communities with cash assistance, food and hygiene supplies across six of the worst-affected districts: Cox’s Bazar, Bandarban, Rangamati, Chittagong, Khagrachari and Moulvibazar.

item.title: Air pollution causes 242 deaths daily
item.description: The city-wise analysis revealed that Dhaka is the most severely affected, with approximately 68,703 deaths related to PM2.5 pollution annually.

item.title: China turns to electric taxis to soften Hormuz oil shock
item.description: With the electrification of taxis, the rideshare boom adds to evidence that transportation in China is becoming less dependent on oil.

item.title: Weather-hit HSC students to sit missed exams later with Chattogram board: Edu Minister
item.description: Across the country, some 1.27 million candidates are taking the HSC and equivalent examinations at 2,697 centres.

item.title: Iran threatens to block more vital seaways as Trump orders renewed Iran blockade
item.description: Analysts have said Iran has been signalling it may use its Houthi allies in Yemen to shut the Bab el-Mandeb gateway to the Red Sea.

item.title: MBA Association calls on finance minister, discusses policy cooperation
item.description: The association reaffirmed its commitment to serve as an independent, unbiased and non-political task force.

item.title: Low-pressure area in Bay of Bengal: BMD forecasts heavy rain in various regions
item.description: Due to adverse weather conditions, the seaports of Chittagong, Cox’s Bazar, Mongla and Payra have been advised to keep the local cautionary signal number 3 hoisted.

item.title: Meeting held to review, finalise draft of Cyber Security (Amendment) Act
item.description: The meeting focused on exchanging views regarding necessary amendments and recommendations aimed at addressing the rapid changes in information technology, strengthening cyber security, protecting the rights of citizens and ensuring a safe environment for digital services.

item.title: Soybean, palm oil imports fall 10% amid price control dispute
item.description: Bangladesh imported 2.22 million tonnes of soybean and palm oil in FY2025-26, leaving supplies below estimated domestic demand.

item.title: DAP recommends 14 eco-parks, 55 water parks to resolve Dhaka’s waterlogging and restore livability
item.description: If water bodies and open green spaces are preserved in a planned manner, it is possible not only to reduce waterlogging in the capital but also to play a major role in temperature control, groundwater recharge and biodiversity conservation, according to urban planners.

item.title: Bangladesh’s youth are South Asia’s most anxious over economic security
item.description: A UNFPA survey found 67.2 percent of young Bangladeshis worried about economic uncertainty, conflict and inequality, higher than their peers elsewhere in South Asia. Globally, Bangladesh ranked tenth among 73 nations. Against this backdrop, Bangladesh is marking World Youth Skills Day on July 15.

item.title: Bangladesh envoy highlights academic diplomacy with book donation to Maldives university
item.description: Maldives National University welcomed the book as a contribution to scholarship on diplomacy and international relations while expressing interest in expanding collaboration with Bangladeshi institutions.

item.title: Warren Buffett cuts off donations to Gates Foundation following Epstein revelations
item.description: The Justice Department’s Epstein files ‌included photos of ⁠Gates posing with the financier and with women whose faces were redacted. Emails showed communications between Epstein and the foundation’s staff.

item.title: Benapole’s secure warehouse fails to protect cargo, hurting trade
item.description: Several customs officials have been detained or suspended as investigations uncover an organised smuggling network operating from the facility.

item.title: Trump resumes Iran port blockade and threatens strikes on energy targets
item.description: U.S. negotiators had been in touch with their Iranian counterparts to tell them “you better make a deal”, Trump ​added.

item.title: Scottish traffic cone gets dignitary’s welcome in Boston after World Cup friendship
item.description: “There are still some traffic cones atop our most important statues,” Boston Mayor Michelle Wu joked Tuesday, recalling how Boston had “unofficially become New Scotland.”

item.title: Spain shuts down France and Kylian Mbappé, advances to the World Cup final with a 2-0 victory
item.description: “We were up against one of the best national teams in the world, but today, they were facing the best team in the world,” Spain coach Luis de la Fuente said.

item.title: DCCI President calls on State Minister for Foreign Affairs
item.description: The DCCI president stressed that diversifying both export products and export destinations would be crucial for Bangladesh to effectively address the post-LDC era challenges.

item.title: Bangladesh, Saudi Arabia hold meeting to strengthen transport, logistics cooperation
item.description: The two sides discussed port infrastructure development, maritime transport, modernisation of logistics systems, expansion of investment opportunities, exchange of skills and technology, and various issues of mutual interest, said a PID handout.

item.title: Frontier joins airlines betting on Starlink to lure travellers
item.description: The Denver-based airline is the first ultra-low-cost U.S. carrier to sign on with Elon Musk’s Starlink as the ​company competes with Amazon’s Kuiper for airline customers ​to provide in-flight Wi-Fi.

item.title: Seven more children die from measles and measles-like symptoms
item.description: In total, 766 children have died from measles and measles-like symptoms so far.

item.title: LGRD minister calls for united efforts to tackle flood situation
item.description: The minister said that every ministry and agency must discharge its responsibilities properly and ensure that services and assistance reach the affected people without delay.

item.title: President stresses equipping youth with future-focused skills to face challenges
item.description: The president called upon government and private organisations, development partners and stakeholders from industry and commerce to work together to build a skills-based work culture and encourage young people to acquire relevant skills.

item.title: Speakers call for integrated initiatives, increased investment to equip youth with future-ready skills
item.description: Stakeholders have emphasised aligning skill development systems with industry demands and the future job market, as well as prioritising upskilling, reskilling, strengthening industry-academia collaboration and expanding training based on new technologies, including artificial intelligence.

item.title: An ‘economic hitman’ seeking to rebuild influence
item.description: Although no court has convicted him, Sarafat remains the subject of ongoing investigations as authorities examine allegations spanning banking, mutual funds and capital markets.

item.title: Govt targets 42.5MW from Dhaka waste-to-energy project, says PM’s adviser
item.description: The Aminbazar plant will process about 3,000 tonnes of waste a day, with electricity supply to the national grid targeted from August 2028.

item.title: PM inaugurates ‘National Startup and Entrepreneurship Platform’
item.description: The platform aims to integrate the innovative ideas, creativity and entrepreneurial potential of the country’s youth into the mainstream of national development.

item.title: Bhomra port misses FY26 revenue target despite higher collections
item.description: Revenue collections at the Bhomra land port reached BDT 11.14 billion against a target of BDT 20.64 billion, rising year-on-year despite a decline in import volumes.

item.title: Judge says Trump IRS lawsuit was filed for ‘improper purpose,’ refers lawyer for possible discipline
item.description: The judge stopped short of explicitly voiding the deal shielding Trump from tax scrutiny but said the government cannot claim in official proceedings that the agreement was the result of a legitimate legal process.

item.title: Three years of flood disruption cast doubt over 2027 HSC, equivalent exams
item.description: Heavy rain has disrupted HSC examinations for three straight years, raising concerns that the 2027 schedule could face similar challenges unless the calendar is revised.

item.title: Extreme heat threatens Italy’s Parmesan heartland
item.description: With temperatures topping 40 degrees Celsius, cows spend more time lying ​down, eat less and produce up to 10 percent less milk, one of the Parmigiano’s only three ingredients alongside ⁠salt and rennet.

item.title: Trump says the US will take out Pickaxe Mountain in Iran
item.description: “We’re going to take out Pickaxe Mountain. Tell the Iranians to be ​ready,” Trump said in an interview on the Hugh ​Hewitt Show.

item.title: No plans to cancel agreement with Denmark for operating Chittagong Port: Shipping Minister
item.description: The minister stated that the agreement was executed through a completely transparent process under the Government-to-Government (G2G) framework between the governments of Bangladesh and Denmark, strictly following the Public-Private Partnership (PPP) Act and relevant policies.

item.title: Intel announces $5.7 billion AI-driven capital investment in Ireland
item.description: Intel said the move would upgrade and maximise capacity at its facility in Leixlip outside Dublin that produces Intel 3 silicon wafers, which the company says is ​the most advanced semiconductor manufacturing facility of its kind in Europe.

item.title: Rohingya strategy committee formed under Prime Minister’s leadership
item.description: The committee will determine priorities on key issues relating to Rohingya repatriation and oversee coordination among relevant ministries, law enforcement agencies and intelligence organisations involved in the process, according to the notification.

item.title: Nearly 33,000 arrested in special police drives
item.description: During this period, they recovered 243 firearms, 2,313 rounds of ammunition and cartridges, 88 magazines, 43 cocktails (crude bombs), 2 kg of gunpowder, 503 local weapons, 18 weapon parts, 17 tools for making weapons and bombs and 10,300 chocolate bombs.

item.title: Floods claim 54 lives across seven districts
item.description: In total, the number of affected people has reached 609,411.

item.title: Money laundering case of around BDT 346 million filed against Flight Expert
item.description: The allegation against the company is that it accepted advance payments from hundreds of customers and sub-agents for air tickets but failed to provide them.

item.title: Government sends medical teams to flood-hit districts, cancels leave
item.description: For flood-affected farmers, the government would provide direct cash grants every year based on verified lists, the health minister added.

item.title: Southern Chattogram submerges amid unplanned infrastructure and occupation of rivers, canals
item.description: The Flood Forecasting & Warning Centre of the Bangladesh Water Development Board has meanwhile stated that water levels in rivers in several districts of the northeastern, northern and southeastern regions may rise and exceed danger levels within the next 24 hours.

item.title: Rajshahi zoo to be converted into international-standard safari park, says state minister
item.description: Speaking after an inspection of the Rajshahi Central Botanical Garden and Zoo, the state minister said the proposal reflected long-standing expectations among local residents.

item.title: PM urges public to take responsibility for keeping country clean
item.description: Speaking at a tree-planting programme in Barisal, Tarique Rahman said every citizen must help protect the environment by keeping their surroundings clean.

item.title: Businesses demand probe into cargo losses at Chattogram port
item.description: Business leaders in a joint letter sent to the shipping minister on Sunday urged an independent investigation into cargo losses, warning that prolonged disruption threatens exports, manufacturers and supply chains.

item.title: HSC candidates in Cumilla wade through floodwater as exams go ahead
item.description: Students in Cumilla reached centres through knee- and waist-deep water while the Chattogram Education Board suspended its HSC examinations because of rain and flooding.

item.title: Dhaka’s lost retention ponds leave city vulnerable to flooding
item.description: Experts warn Dhaka faces worsening flooding and water scarcity after decades of inaction allowed strategic rainwater reservoirs to shrink or disappear.

item.title: Oil jumps 4% as new military strikes threaten Hormuz shipments
item.description: Brent crude futures climbed $3.10, or 4.08 percent, to $79.11 by 0325 GMT, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude rose $2.95, or 4.11 percent, to $74.36 a barrel.

item.title: Iran expands attacks on Gulf states after US strikes, says Strait of Hormuz closed
item.description: Iranian media said on Sunday there had been missile attacks and explosions around the port cities of Sirik and Bandar Abbas, ​home to military facilities on the strait, and nearby Qeshm Island.

item.title: Tarique Rahman makes first official visit to Barisal as PM
item.description: Lawmaker from the Barisal-1 constituency and local BNP leaders welcomed the prime minister as his motorcade reached Gournadi at around 10:00 am.

item.title: Three-day summer school on Bengal’s intellectual history held in Dhaka
item.description: The three-day programme brought together academics, writers and students to examine regional autonomy, colonial encounters and the making of modern Bangladesh.

item.title: Rescued Royal Bengal tiger released into Sundarbans after six-month recovery
item.description: The tigress regained her ability to hunt during six months of veterinary care before being released into the eastern Sundarbans.

item.title: BGB deployed for rescue and relief across 11 flood-hit districts
item.description: The deployment covers districts in the Chattogram Hill Tracts, southeast and northeast as authorities respond to widespread flooding and landslides.

item.title: Heavy rain raises short-term flood risk across northern and eastern districts
item.description: Rivers in Sylhet, Chattogram and northern districts are expected to exceed danger levels at several points over the next two days, according to FFWC.

item.title: Elephant injured after 300-foot fall in Teknaf dies despite treatment
item.description: Forest officials said rain-softened soil gave way as the animal was feeding, sending her tumbling down a hillside with fatal injuries.

item.title: PM holds meeting on Dhaka waste-to-energy projects
item.description: Chinese and South Korean firms will develop separate facilities expected to process up to 6,500 tonnes of waste a day and generate electricity, methane and other by-products.

item.title: Emergency crew rescues four students stranded in Sitakunda Ecopark
item.description: The rescue team, responding to an emergency call to the 999 national hotline, trekked three kilometres through a flooded hill stream in heavy rain to reach the group.

item.title: Dhaka South deploys emergency teams after month’s heaviest rainfall
item.description: The Dhaka South city administrator said field teams had been on duty since dawn to reduce waterlogging and minimise disruption to traffic.

item.title: Finance minister says new IMF programme must safeguard public interest
item.description: Speaking in Dhaka, the minister said any future agreement with the IMF would have to safeguard the interests of the Bangladeshi people and the broader economy.

item.title: Heavy rain and upstream floods inundate aman seedbeds, threatening harvest
item.description: Agricultural experts warned that if floodwaters do not recede quickly, seedbeds could rot, causing farmers to miss the aman transplanting window and potentially reducing rice output, with implications for food security.

item.title: How the absence of Iran’s Mojtaba Khamenei is becoming a liability for the Islamic Republic
item.description: Senior sources in Iran have attributed the lack of any new image or voice recording since his March 8 appointment by a clerical assembly to health and security considerations.

item.title: Singapore sets benchmark while Chattogram battles urban failings
item.description: Chattogram’s strategic location and expanding port infrastructure offer significant potential, but urban planners say lasting progress will require coordinated long-term management rather than project-led development.

item.title: Qatar’s former emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani passes away at 74
item.description: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani ruled Qatar from 1995 ​to 2013, before abdicating to his son Sheikh ​Tamim.

item.title: Kushner project being developed on disputed land, Albanian villagers say
item.description: The villagers’ legal claims complicate what is already ​a contentious multi-billion-euro development on an island and a pristine piece of mainland that includes a protected wetland home to migrating flamingos, seals and sea turtles.

item.title: Bangladesh’s apparel exports to US shrink 8% in January-May
item.description: The decline came despite a 6.04 percent rise in May, when shipments climbed to $582 million.

item.title: Chattogram-Cox’s Bazar rail services to resume after five-day closure
item.description: Services were halted on Tuesday after an estimated 500-metre section of track near Shamsherpara, in the Sholoshohor–Jan Ali Hat segment, was inundated.

item.title: US attacks Iran over ship being hit in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran said that the strait would remain closed “until further notice” and said it would consider targeting “additional enemy bases in the region” if it faced more attacks.

item.title: Former acting president, ex-speaker Jamiruddin Sircar passes away at 95
item.description: The BNP confirmed his death in a brief statement issued by its media cell early on Sunday.

item.title: Argentina overcome Switzerland 3-1 after extra time to reach last four
item.description: Argentina will face England in Atlanta after prevailing in a dramatic quarter-final marked by a rare VAR mistaken-identity correction

item.title: Govt launches extensive flood response campaign under PM’s round-the-clock monitoring: Spokesperson
item.description: The spokesperson said the prime minister has been monitoring the situation around the clock and has directed all relevant ministries, local administrations and law enforcement agencies to remain alongside the affected communities.

item.title: Bangladesh’s demographic dividend nearing its end amid rising elderly population
item.description: According to experts, if this situation persists, the country will face economic pressure related to an ageing population before it can fully capitalise on its demographic dividend.

item.title: Trump says US, Iran agree to continue talks, but ceasefire over
item.description: The United States also stepped up demands on Friday that Iran stop attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz, where recent hostilities have pushed oil prices higher, a politically sensitive issue for Trump ahead of November congressional elections.

item.title: Hundreds of thousands evacuated as Typhoon Bavi barrels towards China
item.description: Even as Bavi continues to slow and weaken on its northwesterly path over cooler seas, the typhoon is still a potent risk due to the sheer volume of moisture it holds within its rain bands, about the size of France from end to end.

item.title: Two-day workshop on bancassurance begins at DU
item.description: The distinguished guests highlighted the importance of bancassurance in advancing financial inclusion, broadening access to insurance services, and ensuring suitable, reliable, and customer-friendly insurance products through the banking network.

item.title: Adviser Mahdi Amin outlines PM’s 10-point directive to tackle disaster in Ctg region
item.description: He said the prime minister had taken prompt and effective steps to bring the situation under control and stand beside the affected people during the crisis.

item.title: Hundreds of thousands of people waterlogged in Cox’s Bazar, 25 dead
item.description: A control room has been opened to address the emergency situation, measures have been taken to open sluice gates for faster water drainage, and 648 shelter centres across the district have been kept ready, according to the administration.

item.title: Farmers losing interest in Boro paddy cultivation due to high costs; Aman yields also failing to increase
item.description: If farmers’ interest in Boro cultivation continues to decline, the country’s food security could face a threat in the future.

item.title: Chinese company CCDC expresses interest in drilling abandoned gas wells
item.description: CCDC has proposed entering into an agreement with Petrobangla under the Production Sharing Contract (PSC) model.

item.title: Gold price increases by BDT 2,216 per bhori
item.description: The Bangladesh Jewellers Association (BAJUS) stated that this adjustment was made due to an increase in the price of pure gold, or tejabi gold, in the local market.

item.title: Eleven killed in fast-moving southern Spain wildfire, officials say
item.description: ‌Andalusia’s head of emergencies said the authorities had told residents to stay in their homes and that the deaths appeared ​to have occurred when people decided to try to evacuate in their cars.

item.title: Special delivery: Italy’s postman joins the AI infrastructure race
item.description: Poste Italiane, the postal service which pays out pensions through 12,600 post offices that are as much a feature of ​remote towns as the local church, is betting on its €13.5 billion ($15.4 billion) bid for Telecom Italia (TIM) to accelerate its shift into digital, telecom and cloud services.

item.title: Iran says it hits U.S. military targets in Gulf, buries slain leader Khamenei
item.description: Iranian media later reported multiple explosions across southern Iran, including Bushehr, where one of the country’s nuclear plants is located, along with Konarak, Choghadak and Bandar Abbas.

item.title: Bangladesh exports 812 products to 202 destinations worldwide: Commerce Minister
item.description: The minister informed the House that Bangladesh earned $44,167.84 million from exports of these products during FY 2024–25.

item.title: Grameenphone introduces ‘Instagram basic mode’ to keep users connected without data
item.description: With ‘Instagram basic mode’, customers can get a limited Instagram browsing experience after their regular data balance is exhausted.

item.title: Minister calls for 50-year masterplan to modernise child development centres
item.description: The minister said long-term planning should transform the country’s child development centres into modern rehabilitation facilities with improved education, healthcare and accommodation.

item.title: Chinese envoy calls on PM’s defence adviser
item.description: Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to working together for peace and stability in the region amid evolving global and regional geopolitical developments.

item.title: Info minister for Dhaka-Cairo joint initiative in combating misinformation
item.description: Egyptian Ambassador to Bangladesh Omar Mohie Eldin Ahmed Fahmy paid a courtesy call on Information Minister Zahir Uddin Swapon.

item.title: SC upholds ruling restoring caretaker govt provisions, referendums
item.description: The ruling marks a significant shift after the court declared provisions abolishing the caretaker government system through the 15th constitutional amendment unlawful.

item.title: Home minister urges future-ready UN Police at UNCOPS summit
item.description: The minister said Bangladesh has undertaken significant reforms to better equip its police force to respond to evolving security challenges.

item.title: Iran to bury slain Supreme Leader in culmination of mass funeral
item.description: Crowds marched through Mashhad on Thursday morning as they waved Iranian flags, photographs ​of the late Khamenei and placards with revolutionary slogans.

item.title: PM urges countrymen to make tree planting a daily habit
item.description: The prime minister was addressing the inauguration ceremony of the World Environment Day programme, Environment Fair 2026 and the National Tree Plantation Campaign and Tree Fair 2026.

item.title: Can Bangladesh’s Islamic banking sector rebuild public trust?
item.description: Deposit withdrawals, soaring bad loans and governance failures have shaken confidence in the country’s Islamic banking industry. Economists warn that restoring trust will take years. The domestic crisis, however, obscures a broader reality: Islamic banking remains a growth story globally.

item.title: Why Trump covets Greenland
item.description: Greenland’s own government opposes U.S. designs on the island, saying the people of Greenland will decide their own future.

item.title: UN probe says mass killings, rapes, abductions, starvation by Sudan force amount to genocide
item.description: The report found ​that the RSF and allies committed the war crime of starvation by imposing a prolonged siege on the city, impeding relief supplies, and shelling food production systems.

item.title: ADB trims Bangladesh growth outlook, cites stubborn inflation
item.description: The Manila-based lender expects growth to recover modestly next fiscal year but warned that banking sector weaknesses and external risks could limit the pace of expansion.

item.title: Chittagong Dry Dock wins in-principle approval for Kanchpur bus terminal work
item.description: CDDL received in-principle approval to construct sheds, ticket counters, toilets, offices and other ancillary structures for the terminal under the Dhaka South City Corporation.

item.title: Trump says ‘a lot of unity’ at NATO summit after lashing out at allies
item.description: On paper at least, the summit ended with a message of solidarity, with NATO allies including Trump affirming their “ironclad commitment” to collective defence under the alliance’s ​Article 5 pact in a summit declaration.

item.title: US military carries out fresh strikes on Iran, prompting Iran attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: Kuwait’s Defense Ministry said it ⁠was intercepting missiles and drones, while Qatar briefly issued an “elevated security threat” alert before later giving the all-clear.

item.title: Catastrophic storms to test China’s resilience in 2026, scientists warn
item.description: China’s National Climate Center expects up ‌to six typhoons to form in the Northwest Pacific and South China Sea in July, more than the average of 3.8.

item.title: China’s Nexchip to raise $890 million in Hong Kong share sale
item.description: Chinese chipmakers are stepping up investment in manufacturing technology and artificial intelligence as Beijing ​pushes for greater semiconductor self-sufficiency.

item.title: Govt firmly committed to peaceful, swift resolve of Rohingya crisis: PM
item.description: The prime minister stated that the BNP government is conducting vigorous diplomatic efforts on bilateral, regional and multilateral fronts to achieve an early resolution of the Rohingya crisis.

item.title: Irene Khan appointed as Bangladesh’s permanent representative to UN
item.description: The notification, signed by Cabinet Secretary Nasimul Gani by order of the president, states that the government has conferred the rank of state minister upon Irene Khan.

item.title: 61% of Probashi Kallyan Bank’s loan portfolio now in default
item.description: Various related sources and Bangladesh Bank’s observations indicate that policymakers at Probashi Kallyan Bank were involved in various irregularities and corruption from the very beginning of its formal journey in 2011.

item.title: Trump turns on Spain and demands Greenland as NATO summit exposes cracks
item.description: Trump’s remarks overshadowed a summit that European leaders had hoped would project unity and support for Ukraine and cap a series of rows that have threatened to tear the military alliance apart.

item.title: Oil jumps over 6% to two-week high after Trump says deal with Iran ‘over’
item.description: At least four oil and gas tankers have turned back from attempting to transit the strait, ship-tracking data showed, as renewed ​attacks on vessels heightened safety concerns.

item.title: Cargo vessel capsizes with 5,000 sacks of rice, paddy in Chandpur
item.description: All six crew members swam to safety after the vessel overturned at the confluence of the Padma, Meghna and Dakatia rivers, with traders estimating losses at around BDT 10 million.

item.title: Children killed in Rohingya camp landslide, 30 reported trapped
item.description: The landslide occurred around 2 pm at the madrasah in sub-block A/3 of camp 5 in Ukhiya.

item.title: Unchecked encroachment eroding Dhaka’s green spaces
item.description: Urban planners stress that parks and open spaces are not luxuries in a densely populated city. But onsite visits across the capital found several parks and playing fields have been turned into parking lots, markets, garages and food courts.

item.title: Ctg-Dohazari rail line to be raised by 5 feet, state minister announces after visit
item.description: Train services to Cox’s Bazar were suspended after torrential rain submerged tracks, prompting plans to elevate the Sholoshohor-Dohazari line to reduce future disruption.

item.title: Iran targets Bahrain and Kuwait after US launches strikes and limits the sale of Iranian oil
item.description: The attacks on shipping and the resulting strikes came during the dayslong funeral for Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed on February 28 in the war’s first moments.

item.title: Bangladesh, Vietnam vow stronger security cooperation to combat human trafficking
item.description: A key focus of the talks was the visa-related difficulties faced by Bangladeshi nationals in Vietnam and the repatriation of irregular migrants.

item.title: Heavy rain puts 4 divisions at risk of flash floods
item.description: Bangladesh’s flood forecasting agency said heavy to very heavy rain over the next 72 hours could trigger flash floods, with river levels expected to rise rapidly in several regions.

item.title: Dhaka ranks among world’s least liveable cities
item.description: Dhaka ranked 171st in the latest livability index. Only Libya’s capital, Tripoli, ranked 172nd, and Syria’s capital, Damascus, in 173rd place, ranked lower.

item.title: Flooded rail tracks disrupt services in Chattogram, four trains cancelled
item.description: A railway source said the combination of track flooding and extended delays caused by operating trains in both directions using connecting rolling stock forced the simultaneous cancellation of all four services.

item.title: Violence escalates in South Sudan as a government commissioner is killed
item.description: The ruling party in a statement said it “strongly condemns the brutal killing” of the commissioner and would hold an emergency session to discuss it.

item.title: Saudi Arabia considers expansion of oil pipeline to Red Sea, sources say

item.description: Egypt had been on the brink of one of the great World Cup upsets after Yasser Ibrahim and Mostafa Zico scored excellent goals.

From the beginning, he encouraged Bangladeshi researchers to lead scientific studies, participate in policy-making, and build institutional capacity.

item.description: The fall in average turnaround time for container vessels was reflected in improved operational efficiency, according to the data.

item.description: Quiñones opened the scoring in the 22nd minute and Jiménez added a goal in the 31st for the Mexicans, who had not won a knockout-stage match since defeating Bulgaria in the round of 16 when they hosted the tournament in 1986.

Consequently, there is no clear information regarding the institutions’ total assets, liabilities, capital, or long-term financial obligations.

item.description: The minister stated that the government is working to make the licensing and approval process easier, faster and more integrated so that investors do not have to move from one office to another.

item.title: Europe swelters under heatwave, France restricts alcohol consumption
item.description: After a crisis meeting, Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu ⁠pre-emptively banned alcohol consumption on Sunday at the annual Fete de la Musique festivals and other ​public events to be held in those 35 regions on Sunday.

item.title: Sada Pathor closes indefinitely amid flash flood risks
item.description: The Companiganj UNO said once the water current subsides and weather conditions stabilise, the decision on reopening the tourist spot will be taken.

item.title: Finance minister signals move towards market-based, alternative financing
item.description: The current FY26 budget will spend about BDT 1.25 trillion on interest payments, the finance minister said, limiting the government’s fiscal space.

item.title: Telegram loses bid to overturn India’s temporary blocking of the app
item.description: The government is “empowered ... to issue directions for blocking the public access to Telegram,” Delhi ​High Court Justice Tejas Karia said in his ruling.

item.title: Global indicator of inefficiency becomes major revenue source for Ctg Port
item.description: Stakeholders believe this situation reflects the long-standing structural limitations of the port and customs systems.

item.title: PM Tarique Rahman departs Dhaka for Malaysia
item.description: The Malaysia tour is expected to focus on trade expansion, labour market cooperation and investment opportunities.

item.title: Report says UK PM Starmer ready to quit, but source says he is still focused on the job
item.description: A government source said Starmer remained focused on his job and pointed to previous statements ​he has made to that effect.

item.title: Top-level US-Iran peace talks to begin at Swiss resort with Hormuz in spotlight
item.description: A halt to fighting in Lebanon was one of the conditions for starting U.S.-Iranian talks on Tehran’s nuclear programme and other ​issues. But Lebanese civil defence officials said Israeli strikes had killed 20 people on Saturday, hours after a truce took effect.

item.title: Five youths stranded for eight days on Kurigram border zero line
item.description: The incident began on June 14 when India’s Border Security Force (BSF) attempted to push six people at Goytapara and three youths at Bhondurchar, according to local accounts.

item.title: 12th Int’l Yoga Day celebrated in Dhaka with healthy ageing theme
item.description: The event followed a series of pre-Yoga Day activities held across Bangladesh in collaboration with educational, media and cultural institutions.

item.title: CAAB launches national civil aviation master plan
item.description: The proposed CAMP will serve as a comprehensive national roadmap for the aviation sector for the next 20 to 30 years and beyond.

item.title: PM heading to Malaysia and China on six-day visit
item.description: Officials expect the prime minister’s first overseas trip since taking office in February this year to yield a broad package of agreements as Bangladesh seeks closer economic and diplomatic ties with two key Asian partners.

item.title: US Vice President JD Vance arrives in Switzerland to launch talks with Iran on its nuclear program
item.description: Top U.S. and Iranian negotiators are in a 60-day sprint to reach an agreement on the technical details that hold massive implications for the world economy and global security.

item.title: Government borrowing from banks triples while private-sector credit flow falls
item.description: Economists and business leaders warn that heavy government reliance on bank financing is worsening a prolonged credit squeeze on private enterprises.

item.title: Undav scores twice as Germany beats Ivory Coast 2-1, advances to World Cup knockout phase
item.description: Undav scored twice as Germany defeated Ivory Coast 2-1 on Saturday and clinched a spot in the knockout phase at the World Cup.

item.title: Pink boots are everywhere at the World Cup as the color becomes a soccer star
item.description: Pink itself does not make players perform any better, but shoe company executives consider it a mindset.

item.title: Curacao’s Eloy Room makes 15 saves, sends island nation to 0-0 draw with Ecuador in World Cup
item.description: The 37-year-old Room, whose shutout of Jamaica last November sent Curacao to its first World Cup, bounced back in historic fashion from a 7-1 loss to Germany.

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 20 in Lebanon hours after ceasefire
item.description: Lebanon's National News Agency reported that Israeli warplanes and drones had struck multiple locations in southern ​Lebanon and the Bekaa Valley.

item.title: US disputes Iranian claims about closing Strait of Hormuz as negotiators head to Switzerland
item.description: Although the U.S. and Iran had agreed to a 60-day ceasefire while negotiations take place, Tehran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps on Saturday declared the Strait of Hormuz shut.

item.title: Patenga terminal adds 4 custom gantry cranes
item.description: The supplier will install and commission the cranes at the jetty, with operations expected to begin by mid-July.

item.title: An Israeli strike hits a Gaza City apartment, killing 2 children, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: The Israeli military didn’t immediately issue a statement, but said it was looking into the incident.

item.title: PM calls for united efforts to harness maritime potential
item.description: The prime minister said in the global reality of the twenty-first century, the ocean is not merely a part of geographical boundaries; rather, it is one of the principal foundations of economic growth, trade connectivity, energy security, environmental balance and national strategic capability.

item.title: Govt to form panels to review CPI, GDP methodologies: State Minister
item.description: The state minister for planning said the review aims to improve the accuracy, transparency and credibility of official economic statistics.

item.title: Tea production climbs 130% in strong start to 2026
item.description: Growers expanded output after a recovery in auction prices improved profitability, reversing two years of production shortfalls.

item.title: US, Iran envoys head for talks, Israeli strikes continue after ceasefire
item.description: Just hours after a ceasefire took effect in Lebanon between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, Israeli air strikes and drone attacks killed at least five people in the south on Saturday, Lebanese state media said.

item.title: Bangladesh renews call for Rohingya repatriation at UN
item.description: Speaking at a UN briefing, Bangladesh Permanent Representative to the United Nations Ambassador Salahuddin Noman Chowdhury said the Rohingya crisis originated in Myanmar and its durable solution must also be found there.

item.title: Animal activists in Dhaka plan human chain to protest stray dog culling calls
item.description: Bangladesh’s officially approved method for managing stray dogs is Catch-Neuter-Vaccinate-Release, an internationally recognised approach, the organisers said.

item.title: Dhaka, Beijing to sign 15-17 instruments during PM’s visit, says foreign secretary
item.description: The prime minister is scheduled to travel to China on Monday afternoon at the invitation of Chinese Premier Li Qiang.

item.title: Border district Thakurgaon carries growing geopolitical relevance
item.description: Thakurgaon’s proximity to India’s Siliguri Corridor and its history of producing national political figures have given the north-western district an outsized role in Bangladesh’s political and strategic landscape.

item.title: Pentagon chief’s review appears out of step with what NATO allies are already doing
item.description: Hegseth does not attend many NATO meetings, and he left Thursday’s gathering of defense ministers early. But his first major speech to the allies in February 2025 and his follow-up this week were memorable.

item.title: Bangladesh Bank moves to tighten SWIFT security with AML tools
item.description: The central bank is adding anti-money laundering tools to its SWIFT defences. The technology automatically suspends suspect transactions the moment they arise, placing all international transfers under heightened scrutiny.

item.title: Paraguay’s Miguel Almiron becomes 1st player red-carded at the World Cup for covering his mouth
item.description: The infraction came during first-half stoppage time in the Group D match when Almiron and Mert Mulder exchanged words following a foul near midfield.

item.title: US reaches World Cup knockout round and wins its group with 2nd straight victory, 2-0 over Australia
item.description: As the Group D winner, the U.S. will play a round-of-32 match on July 1 in Santa Clara, California, against a third-place group finisher.

item.title: PM’s Malaysia visit to prioritise labour pact changes, lower migration costs
item.description: Bangladesh will seek changes to an existing memorandum of understanding that officials say has hindered transparent recruitment, as Prime Minister Tarique Rahman travels to Malaysia this week.

item.title: Matheus Cunha scores 2 goals as Brazil eliminates Haiti from World Cup with 3-0 win
item.description: Haiti, the Western Hemisphere’s poorest nation that qualified for the World Cup for the first time since 1974, became the first team guaranteed not to reach the knockout round of the expanded 48-team tournament.

item.title: Bangladesh seeks deeper UN Women cooperation
item.description: The state minister made the appeal during a bilateral meeting in New York with Sima Bahous, the executive director of UN Women.

item.title: BAJUS cuts gold prices for second straight day
item.description: The new rate of BDT 226,340 per bhori, including value-added tax, took effect at 10 am local time, BAJUS said in a statement.

item.title: Ukrainian drone makers target Asia as Taiwan tensions spur demand
item.description: Ukrainian defense firms like UFORCE, Skyeton and General Cherry hope to find production partners in Japan, ​an industrial heavyweight that this year cast off longstanding restrictions on arms exports.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni says Trump ‘totally invented’ story that she begged him for photo
item.description: Underscoring how much Trump’s ​comments have angered Meloni’s government, Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani pulled out of a visit to the U.S. next week.

item.title: Iranian Guards’ business empire to win big if US sanctions lifted
item.description: The interim deal announced this week will allow waivers on sanctioned oil sales while any more comprehensive agreement struck in the coming period could lift all other sanctions and ​give Iran access to a $300-billion reconstruction fund.

item.title: Bangladesh lose T20I series to Australia
item.description: The Tigers lost the three-match series 2-0.

item.title: Kuakata beach losing shoreline, scenic beauty due to erosion, mismanagement
item.description: Kuakata beach, in particular, has experienced severe erosion since 2010. Since then, more than 2,000 metres of beachfront have been lost to the sea.

item.title: Bangladeshi deposits in Swiss banks rise by 41.5% in one year
item.description: If a Bangladeshi deposits funds using another nationality or conceals their identity, such funds are not included in this data, according to the Swiss central bank.

item.title: China tightens indium export checks as AI demand increases
item.description: China produces nearly 70 percent of the world’s indium, a byproduct of zinc refining mostly used in displays and solder, but also the raw material for making indium phosphide, used to make high-speed optical ​chips for AI data centres.

item.title: India’s RMZ to ramp up data centre capacity with $35 billion push, exec says
item.description: The Bengaluru-headquartered company, which currently has 250 megawatts of capacity, is in the final stages of discussions for three data centre projects that would take its total capacity to more than 1 gigawatt, Deepak Chhabria, president of RMZ Infrastructure, told Reuters in an interview on Thursday.

item.title: Gold and silver prices fall in domestic market
item.description: In a notice issued this morning, BAJUS said the new prices came into effect at 10:00 am.

item.title: Drone strikes beyond the battlefield pump up market for technology to repel them
item.description: The field is drawing billions of dollars of investment, moving well beyond military use to sectors including energy, shipping, data ​centres, hotels and airports.

item.title: New govt medical colleges approved before resolving crises at existing ones
item.description: Experts argue that deficiencies in fundamental facilities create significant obstacles to producing competent physicians in the medical sector, adding that shortcomings are preventing the sector from adequately preparing for future challenges.

item.title: Puppetry pioneer Mustafa Monwar under intensive care in Dhaka
item.description: The artist earned national recognition for his creative children’s programming on the state broadcaster Bangladesh Television (BTV), which shaped the childhoods of successive generations.

item.title: Padma erosion threatens Daulatdia ferry jetties; hundreds of families at risk
item.description: Locals said geo-bag dumping during the monsoon had not fully prevented erosion, arguing that limited preventive work in the dry season reduced its effectiveness.

item.title: Luis Díaz sparks Colombia to a 3-1 win over Uzbekistan in its World Cup opener
item.description: With the White Wolves’ defeat, three of the four debuting teams at this year’s World Cup lost their first match.

item.title: Kane draws level with Lineker as England outgun Croatia
item.description: The Group L meeting carried heavy expectations long before kickoff, given the history between the sides, with Croatia’s extra-time victory over England in the 2018 ‌World Cup semi-finals adding an extra edge to the occasion.

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal held to a 1-1 draw by Congo at the World Cup
item.description: Playing in front of a crowd of 68,777 spectators at NRG Stadium, which was mostly filled with Portugal supporters, Congo earned its first point at the World Cup after losing all three of its previous matches.

item.title: US awards $500 million to Nvidia-backed SandboxAQ for finding new chipmaking materials
item.description: The award is part of President Donald Trump’s move to allocate research funds under the CHIPS Act, an effort that previously funded a $150 million investment in new chip manufacturing tools and a $2 billion investment in quantum computing.

item.title: Bangladesh’s budget: The real deficit is institutional
item.description: The debate surrounding the FY 2026–27 budget has focused heavily on the fiscal deficit, revenue targets, and growth projections. But it overlooks a fundamental question: can Bangladesh translate public expenditure into public value?

item.title: Musk says SpaceX could bring $1 trillion in revenue by 2030
item.description: SpaceX on Friday became the sixth-largest U.S. firm, cementing Musk’s status as the world’s first ​trillionaire.

item.title: In Brazil’s Cerrado region, Indigenous fire practices reshape wildfire strategy
item.description: The Xerente live in the Cerrado, a savanna in central and northern Brazil. Every year, villages face the threat of large-scale forest fires, a danger likely to worsen with the arrival of El Niño, a phenomenon that prolongs drought and pushes regional temperatures higher.

item.title: “Bangladesh’s first recycling project for waste management to be launched in Khulna”
item.description: “Khulna City Corporation and the Asian Development Bank are jointly implementing the project with financial support from the latter. The project has an allocated budget of BDT 750 million.”

item.title: “LPG now a clean, safe and reliable fuel source for millions of Bangladeshi households”
item.description: “Initially, LPG was seen by many as an alternative fuel. Today, it has become the primary cooking fuel for millions of households across the country. Consumers now place greater importance on its convenience, cleanliness, safety and reliability.”

item.title: “At Petromax, safety is not just a compliance requirement but a core responsibility”
item.description: “At Petromax, we view safety not as a compliance requirement but as a core responsibility. By embedding safety into every stage of our operations — from import to end-user delivery — we aim to build trust, protect lives and ensure the sustainable growth of the LPG sector in Bangladesh.”

item.title: “United Aygaz has made the LPG market more competitive”
item.description: “Aygaz’s 65 years of global experience and international sourcing network. We believe that with the entry of United Aygaz, the market has become even more competitive. Competition keeps our competitors sharp, and their strength keeps us sharp as well. It is healthy — good for consumers, distributors and the companies themselves.”

item.title: War as strategy: How Iran’s authoritarian regime instrumentalises conflict for domestic control
item.description: Authoritarian regimes are structurally constrained by an inherent deficit of democratic legitimacy, given the absence of free, competitive, and institutionalised electoral processes. Consequently, they must rely on alternative sources of legitimation, including nationalist narratives, ideological framing, performance-based claims, and coercive capacity.

item.title: Rethinking fiscal governance: A blueprint for Bangladesh’s tax future
item.description: Fiscal reform in Bangladesh must go beyond revenue targets. It is not enough to chase numbers; the system must be rebuilt to foster trust and voluntary compliance. By embracing transparency, democracy, prestige, agility, and empathy, the country can unlock a new wave of civic participation.

item.title: Beyond relief: rethinking the Rohingya response
item.description: The Rohingya response is no longer only a humanitarian challenge. It is also a question of long-term policy, regional stability, and shared responsibility. Continuing to meet immediate needs remains essential, but it must be complemented by forward-looking approaches that expand opportunities and reduce dependency over time.

item.title: “A national budget should serve as a policy document for economic transformation”
item.description: “The fundamental weakness is that we still treat the budget essentially as an income-expenditure ledger. A national budget should serve as a policy document for economic transformation. It should spell out which sectors the government will prioritise over the coming year…”

item.title: Runa Laila to receive ‘Minar-E-Dilli’ honour
item.description: The honour will be presented to her at the 15th Delhi International Film Festival in Delhi.

item.title: Asha Bhosle, one of India’s most versatile Bollywood singers, passes away at 92
item.description: She was admitted to the hospital on late Saturday with a chest infection and exhaustion, her granddaughter Zanai Bhosle said in a social media post.

item.title: An extraordinary story of survival retold on screen
item.description: Each day brought uncertainty and the film brings that experience to the big screen.

item.title: A major milestone for Bangladesh’s cinema industry
item.description: Competing against 11 other features in the Big Screen Competition at the International Film Festival Rotterdam (IFFR), the Bangladeshi film “Master” has earned its place among the world’s most prestigious film festivals.

item.title: Bangladeshi folk film ‘Behula Dorodi’ to screen at international festivals
item.description: Jahidul Islam, the producer, said he aims to establish Bengali cinema as a viable commercial proposition internationally while preserving “dying folk traditions” for a new generation through the film.
